senses.gloss: to almost do something (with following non-past or with أَن (ʔan) + subjunctive)

examples.english: It is as if the lightning almost snatched away their sight.

senses.gloss: to hardly do something (negated, with following non-past)

examples.english: They slaughtered her, but they hardly brought themselves to do it.

senses.gloss: as soon as (negated, with following non-past)
to plan or intend to commit crime

etymology.text: A root ك و د (k w d) hasn’t developed. The term is suggested as a borrowing from Old South Arabian 𐩫𐩵 (kd, “to almost do”, form I), related to 𐩫𐩵 (kd, “to treacherously endanger; to start a prosecution against”, form II).
